data.householdPolicy: Adult men only; no minors, no women, no couples.
data.operatorNotes: Faith-based shelter averaging 170+ stays per day across shelter plus transitional beds. Intake gate includes breathalyzer screening at the door and chapel attendance expected during stay — in practice this filters out roughly a third of would-be intakes with active substance use. Beds show available but are not accessible for the unsheltered population who fail the entry conditions.

data.householdPolicy: Daytime drop-in only; site has no overnight beds.
data.operatorNotes: Primary daytime hub on the near-downtown shelter corridor. Walk-in welcome: meals, showers, laundry, mail service, case management, and VI-SPDAT on-site. Reports 80-120 unique daily contacts. Has no overnight capacity, so anyone who walks in after 4 PM is re-routed — a recurring failure point in the current referral chain.

data.householdPolicy: Unaccompanied youth only; Drop-In Center serves anyone age 24 or younger.
data.operatorNotes: Community's only 24-hour low-barrier emergency shelter for youth. Accepts self-referral, law-enforcement drop-off, and agency referral. 30+ day program with case management toward housing, education, and employment goals. Co-located Drop-In Center offers food, hygiene, WiFi, transit to behavioral-health appointments. Roughly one in three exits lack a confirmed next placement because transitional-housing slots downstream are saturated.

data.householdPolicy: Unaccompanied youth; parenting youth with one child accepted.
data.operatorNotes: Apartment-style transitional living for youth aging out of foster care or escaping homelessness. Up to 18 months. Active case plan participation is a condition of stay. Only eight units exist in the entire market — waiting list averages six weeks and realistic admits run 1-2 per quarter.

data.householdPolicy: Families with at least one minor child; no single adults.
data.operatorNotes: Emergency family shelter with a 90-day stay limit. Entry only through 211 coordinated screening or a partner-agency referral. Background check is blocking: any adult with a recent felony conviction bounces the whole family back into the queue, which can loop families through 211 for weeks.

data.householdPolicy: Families with children; scattered-site host-congregation and apartment model.
data.operatorNotes: Capacity is counted in families rather than beds. Uses host-congregation rotations plus standalone apartments. Case management tilts hard toward employment and savings milestones. Average stay 60-90 days. In practice the model works best for families with at least one employable adult — harder match for medically fragile or multi-child single-parent households.

data.householdPolicy: Singles and families served through parallel programs, not combined.
data.operatorNotes: Transitional housing plus rapid-rehousing rental assistance. Referrals flow through coordinated entry. Also operates a separate emergency-financial-assistance program for rent/utilities that is often the last stop before an eviction notice turns into a PIT-count tally.

data.householdPolicy: Referral and resource hub, not a shelter — no beds on site.
data.operatorNotes: One-stop hub co-located with adjacent emergency shelters, renamed from 'Multi-Agency Center' on 2025-05-20. Operates as the community's intended coordinated-entry front door. Real-time HMIS bed matching is still coming online; most handoffs today are phone-based between on-site partner staff and shelter intake teams.

data.householdPolicy: Singles and families in permanent supportive housing units.
data.operatorNotes: HumanKind Ministries' permanent supportive-housing campus. Housing First — no sobriety condition, no treatment compliance requirement. Rent scaled to 30% of income, which is often zero at move-in. On-site clinical case management and behavioral-health services. Waitlist runs 8-12 weeks and is the dominant bottleneck on the permanent-housing side of the system.

data.providerName: Mental Health Association of South Central Kansas
data.householdPolicy: Adults with serious mental illness; singles only.
data.operatorNotes: Supportive housing paired with wraparound psychiatric care. Admission requires a COMCARE clinical assessment, which adds 2-4 weeks to any intake timeline. Works well for the population it serves; is mis-used as an 'anywhere that will take them' referral for anyone visibly in crisis, which creates a steady stream of declined referrals.
